commit.message:
feat: add daily discovery workflow for templates and extensions (#752)

* feat: add daily discovery workflow for templates and extensions

Add automated GitHub Actions workflow that runs daily to discover new azd
templates (repos with azure.yaml) and extensions (repos with registry.json)
on GitHub.

Template discovery:
- Searches GitHub Code Search API for azure.yaml files
- Filters out forks, archived repos, and low-quality matches
- Uses GitHub Models AI (gpt-4o-mini) for quality assessment and metadata generation
- Creates individual PRs for team review (human-in-the-loop)

Extension discovery:
- Searches for registry.json files with extension structure
- Validates using existing validate-extension.js script
- Creates individual PRs matching existing extension-submission pattern

Features:
- discovery-ignore.json for rejected repos (with justification)
- Mermaid flow diagrams in every PR for transparency
- Review checklists in PR descriptions
- Deduplication against existing entries and open PRs
- Rate limiting to respect GitHub API limits

feat: CCB Node.js reimplementation — 6-phase TDD complete (170 tests)

Phase 1 - Foundation:
  - lib/protocol.js: CCG_DONE v2 protocol (reqId-based, anti-crosstalk)
  - lib/terminal.js: tmux abstraction (pure command builders + TmuxBackend)
  - lib/registry.js: pane registry with 7-day TTL
  - lib/project_id.js: project ID via SHA256 hash
  - lib/lock.js: PID file-based mutex
  - lib/start_config.js: .ccg/ccg.config reader (CSV + JSON)

Phase 2 - Daemon Architecture:
  - lib/daemon/server.js: TCP daemon (echo/tmux dual mode)
  - lib/daemon/client.js: daemon client (ping/ask)
  - lib/daemon/worker.js: serial task queue (per-session)
  - lib/daemon/session.js: CCG_DONE polling + reply extraction

Phase 3 - One-click start:
  - bin/ccg-cmd.js: v2 subcommand entry (start/ask/ping/status/new/ctx-transfer)
  - ccg.js: route new subcommands to bin/ccg-cmd.js

Phase 4 - Memory/Context Transfer:
  - lib/memory/session_parser.js: Claude JSONL session parser
  - lib/memory/formatter.js: message formatter (Markdown)
  - lib/memory/deduper.js: message deduplication
  - lib/memory/transfer.js: context migration orchestrator

Phase 5-6 - Tools + Completion Hook:
  - lib/completion_hook.js: result injection via CCG_CALLER env
  - lib/ping.js / lib/status.js: ping/status formatters
  - lib/router.js: subcommand parser

Tests: 170 passing, 0 failing (TDD red→green cycle)

commit.message:
fix(safety): quarantine fabricated PIDs from active dispatch (#65)

* fix(safety): quarantine fabricated PIDs from active dispatch

Remove fabricated/speculative PIDs from device dispatch paths:

- OpenFFBoard 0xFFB1: not registered on pid.codes (HTTP 404), absent
  from firmware repository (GitHub code search: 0 results), not in
  linux-steering-wheels. Completely fabricated.

- VRS 0xA356 (DFP V2), 0xA357 (Pedals V1), 0xA358 (Pedals V2),
  0xA359 (Handbrake), 0xA35A (Shifter): sequential guesses with zero
  external evidence. VRS uses non-sequential PIDs (DFP=0xA355,
  Pedals=0xA3BE, R295=0xA44C), strongly undermining the assumption.
  Not found in Linux kernel hid-ids.h, linux-steering-wheels,
  simracing-hwdb, or any other external source.

Constants retained in protocol crates for documentation/reference.
Dispatch tables, capability resolution, and tests updated.

Evidence sources checked:
- pid.codes registry
- OpenFFBoard firmware (Ultrawipf/OpenFFBoard)
- Linux kernel hid-ids.h (mainline)
- JacKeTUs/linux-steering-wheels compatibility table
- JacKeTUs/simracing-hwdb device database

commit.message:
fix: correct originalPrompt parameter in reflect loop exit drain (#281)

Follow-up to #275. The exit drain in `SendViaOrchestratorReflectAsync`
passes `originalPrompt: prompt` (the outer loop prompt) instead of
`originalPrompt: leftover` (the actual message being sent). This causes
the response to be misattributed in chat history — the user sees their
queued message associated with the wrong original input.

One-line fix identified in Round 6 review (R6-N1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: isolate PromptLibraryService from real filesystem in tests (#277)

Follow-up to #272.

Fixes a test isolation gap where `PromptLibraryService` could read/write
the real `~/.polypilot/prompts/` directory during tests, and a parallel
race between `PromptCommandTests` and `PromptLibraryTests` causing flaky
failures.

**Changes:**
- Add `PromptLibraryService.SetUserPromptsDirForTesting()` to
`TestSetup` module initializer so tests always start with an isolated
temp dir (matches the pattern for `CopilotService` and `RepoManager`)
- Add `[Collection("PromptLibrary")]` to both `PromptCommandTests` and
`PromptLibraryTests` to prevent parallel mutation of the process-global
`_userPromptsDir` static field
- Add `PromptLibraryCollection.cs` with the `[CollectionDefinition]`

Without this fix, tests that access
`PromptLibraryService.UserPromptsDir` before an explicit
`SetUserPromptsDirForTesting` call resolve to the real filesystem path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skill-install.sh fails silently when only one directory is detected

When DIRS has exactly 1 element, the code set CHOICE='1' and printed
'Installing to: ...' but never called install_to_directory(). The actual
install only happened in the else-branch (multiple dirs path).

test: harden mirror resume-from-journal with edge case tests (#485)

Add 12 edge case tests for MirrorEngine resume-from-journal behavior:
- Resume after crash during download (skip already-downloaded files)
- Resume after crash during rename (overwrite partial temp files)
- Orphan temp file cleanup via SQLiteTransferJournal.clearStaleTemps
- Multiple resume passes (each makes incremental progress)
- Journal corruption detection (corrupt DB throws, fresh DB works)

- File modification detection via diff between generations
- Storage full simulation (journal.complete throws, failure recorded)
- Empty source folder (no crash, empty report)
- Journal WAL orphan marking on init (active→failed on restart)
- Partial success journal accuracy (all calls tracked)
- Folder-only device (no files to download, no crash)

commit.message:
docs: MTP compatibility research from libmtp device flags analysis (#484)

Analyze libmtp's 32 device flags and map them to SwiftMTP's QuirkFlags.
Add comprehensive research document covering:
- All libmtp device flag definitions and their purpose
- Coverage analysis showing which flags SwiftMTP handles vs is missing
- Common failure patterns by manufacturer (Samsung, Pixel, Xiaomi, Canon, SanDisk)
- Composite bug profiles (Android, Sony NWZ, Aricent)
- Recommendations for auto-detection by vendor extension string

Add 9 new QuirkFlags based on the analysis:
- forceResetOnClose: USB reset after session close (Android, Sony NWZ)
- noZeroReads: no zero-length USB packet termination (Samsung, iRiver)
- noReleaseInterface: skip interface release on close (SanDisk)
- ignoreHeaderErrors: tolerate broken PTP headers (Creative, Aricent)
- brokenSendObjectPropList: fall back to SendObjectInfo (all Android)
- brokenSetObjectPropList: fall back to SetObjectPropValue (Android)
- skipCloseSession: don't close session on exit (Canon EOS 2016+)
- propListOverridesObjectInfo: prefer proplist over ObjectInfo (Samsung)
- samsungPartialObjectBoundaryBug: avoid 512-byte boundary reads (Samsung)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
transport: implement IOUSBHost bulk transfer MTP operations (#481)

Implement the remaining MTP operations for the IOUSBHost native transport:
- getStorageInfo: parse storage type, capacity, free space, description
- getObjectHandles: parse uint32 array of object handles
- getObjectInfos: batch-fetch object metadata (format, size, name, parent)
- getObjectInfos(storage:parent:format:): convenience wrapper
- moveObject: send MoveObject (0x100E) via bulk OUT
- copyObject: send CopyObject (0x101A), return new handle from response
- Data-out phase in executeStreamingCommand for sendObject support

All operations follow the same PTP container pattern as the libusb transport:
build command container, send via bulk OUT, read response via bulk IN.

Update tests: replace notImplemented expectations with invalidState checks
for implemented methods, add comprehensive parsing tests for storage IDs,
object handles, storage info, and PTP container encoding.

* fix(env): load dotenv before imports to fix Shopify env vars

CRITICAL FIX: Moved dotenv.config() to top of file before any imports.
This ensures SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN and other env vars are loaded before
the shopify.ts module reads them at import time.

Issue: shopify.ts reads process.env at module-level (const SHOPIFY_DOMAIN)
which happened BEFORE dotenv loaded .env.local, causing 'MISSING_STORE_DOMAIN'

Solution: Import dotenv first, call config() immediately, then import other modules

Fixes #31

* fix(shopify): read env vars at runtime instead of module load time

CRITICAL FIX: Shopify env vars were being read at module-level (cached)
before dotenv loaded them, causing MISSING_STORE_DOMAIN errors.

Changes:
- Created getShopifyConfig() to read env vars at runtime
- Updated all functions to call getShopifyConfig() instead of using cached constants
- Ensures env vars are always fresh from process.env

This fixes the issue where debug logs showed vars were SET but Shopify
integration said they were MISSING.

commit.message:
Create CorpAI V1 POC project structure

Maven multi-module Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2.3 project with:
- corpai-common: shared DTOs and JPA entities
- corpai-gateway: Spring Cloud Gateway on port 8079
- corpai-orchestrator: main SIB-zone orchestration service on port 8080
- corpai-report-generator: iText7 PDF post-processing library
- corpai-azure-function: standalone Azure Function for AI report generation

Key features:
- PII stays in SIB zone (CRBR data never sent to Azure)
- DataSanitizer converts names to initials before Azure call
- Async processing with @Retryable Azure Function calls
- PostgreSQL persistence for analyses and PDF reports
- iText7 PDF generation and CRBR section appending

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
